judgment.summary: Zoom maintains highly profitable margins and a massive cash cushion despite slowing revenue growth and negative analyst outlooks.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS was 1.44 vs an estimate of 1.49 (miss), with the next earnings date on 2026-05-21.
positives: Total cash $7.82B vs total debt $58.48M — represents a very strong balance sheet.
Free cash flow (TTM) $1.96B — shows robust cash generation.
Gross margin 77.4% — indicates high efficiency in service delivery.
negatives: Revenue growth 5.3% — indicates slowing top-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates for current Q (-0.9%) and FY (-0.8%) are negative.
PEG ratio 4.21 — suggests valuation is high relative to growth.
Last reported EPS 1.44 vs estimate 1.49 — a 3.1% miss.

news.paragraph: Zoom has recently faced downward pressure, with shares plummeting more steeply than the broader market. However, Citigroup has offset some of this negativity by raising its price target to $122 and maintaining a buy rating. Traders should monitor the upcoming Q1 earnings report as a primary catalyst for near-term volatility.
